codeReview.summary: The goods-home-page feature is well-implemented and covers all requirements from the feature description. The banner carousel auto-advances every 4 seconds with swipe support and dot indicators. The search bar filters products in real-time. The product grid uses a 2-column layout with proper price formatting ($XXX.XX). Product cards are clickable and navigate to /products/[id]. The floating customer service icon is positioned correctly and doesn't crash on tap. The API endpoint supports ?q= filtering (case-insensitive). Empty state displays 'No products found'. Mobile-first layout is applied with proper pb-20 for tab bar clearance. Tests are comprehensive with 4 API tests and 12 component tests. All checks (tests, typecheck, lint) pass. Minor non-blocking issues noted around type duplication, the unused server-side search path from the UI, and missing error state UI.

issues.description: Product interface is defined identically in 3 files (app/page.tsx:10, components/goods/ProductCard.tsx:5, components/goods/ProductGrid.tsx:5). Should use a shared type definition, either from Prisma generated types or a shared types file in types/. This violates DRY and creates maintenance risk if the Product model changes.

When a search query is provided, ALL products are fetched from the database and then filtered in JavaScript (lines 20-26). This is a valid workaround for SQLite's lack of case-insensitive LIKE, but won't scale with large datasets. Acceptable for current seed data size (6+ products).

The page fetches all products on mount without ?q= parameter, then filters client-side in the useEffect (line 43-50). The API's ?q= search feature exists but is never used by the actual UI — only exercised by tests. The real-time search requirement is met via client-side filtering, but the API search capability is redundant in practice. Not a bug, but a design inconsistency.

If fetch('/api/products') fails, the error is caught and logged to console, but no error state is shown to the user. The loading spinner disappears and the grid renders empty, which could be confused with 'no products found'. Consider adding an error state with a retry button.
Uses plain HTML tags instead of Next.js Image component. Lint warnings confirm this. Acceptable for external placeholder URLs (picsum.photos) but loses Next.js image optimization benefits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker defined the same Product interface in 3 separate files (app/page.tsx, components/goods/ProductCard.tsx, components/goods/ProductGrid.tsx) instead of using a shared type. AGENTS.md doesn't document a convention for shared type definitions or whether to use Prisma-generated types vs manual interfaces for client components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/page.tsx:10, components/goods/ProductCard.tsx:5, components/goods/ProductGrid.tsx:5 — all define identical { id: string; name: string; price: number; imageUrl: string } interface

sharedStateObservations.observation: The middleware matcher '/((?!api/auth|_next|favicon.ico|robots.txt|.*\\..*$).*)' protects /api/products behind authentication. AGENTS.md doesn't document which API routes require auth vs which should be public. Currently all non-auth API routes are protected, which is fine but should be explicit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: middleware.ts:47 — matcher pattern; app/api/products/route.ts has no auth logic because middleware handles it

summary: PASS. The goods-home-page feature fully implements all 7 expected behaviors: banner carousel with auto-advance and swipe, product grid in 2-column layout with proper price formatting, real-time search filtering, empty state message, clickable product cards with /products/[id] navigation, floating customer service icon, and the GET /api/products endpoint with ?q= search. All 46 tests pass (including 4 API + 12 component tests added by this feature), typecheck clean, lint clean (only img warnings). Manual verification via agent-browser confirmed all interactive behaviors. Five non-blocking issues noted: duplicate Product type definitions, in-memory search filtering approach, unused server-side search from UI, missing fetch error state UI, and use of plain img tags. None are blocking — the feature is complete and correct.
